subject: Weird behavior after 3D denoising of reconstructed tomograms
body:
Dear all,
I've been testing Warp's 3D denoising of tomograms, I've got it running 
successfully, and the command I use is:
noise2map.exe --observation1 even --observation2 odd 
--dont_flatten_spectrum --iterations 800 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 
--gpuid_preprocess 0.

I'm using 800 iterations in order to see whether this program could be 
running properly, but the output tomogram have a really strange look, as 
attached, does anybody know why the output looks like this?

Thank you very much in advance!

Cheers,
Linhua Tai
